en.commentary: I put the Dreo Pilot Max S at number one because it delivers the quiet bladeless experience most people actually want at a price that undercuts the prestige names by hundreds of dollars. Its brushless DC motor bottoms out at 25dB, which is genuinely quieter than a whisper across a bedroom, and the 12 speeds give you the granularity to dial in exactly the airflow you want before sleep. App, Alexa, and Google control all work without a paywall. Dyson's TP10 stays my pick when air quality matters, because the sealed HEPA H13 filter and Air Multiplier projection do something no plain fan can, and the build quality is in its own league. I rank Shark's TurboBlade third for sheer versatility, since pivoting it horizontal to push air 80 feet down a hallway is a trick the others cannot match. Levoit's Classic 42 is the smart fan to buy when budget is tight, and Vornado earns its spot for moving whole-room air through vortex circulation rather than a narrow beam. For a hot Taiwan summer, the honest answer is that a $130 Dreo or Levoit cools a bedroom just as well as a fan costing three times more.

highlights.title: The Dreo Pilot Max S is the tower fan to buy in 2026
highlights.body: It hits 25dB on its quiet DC motor, offers 12 speeds for precise control, and bundles app plus Alexa and Google voice at around a third of Dyson's price. For the overwhelming majority of buyers who want a quiet, capable bedroom fan, paying more buys diminishing returns.

highlights.title: Dyson's TP10 is worth it only when you need purification
highlights.body: The sealed HEPA H13 filter captures allergens and fine particles while it cools, which is a real advantage during pollen season or in a dusty room. If you just want airflow, the gap between it and a $130 smart tower does not justify the premium.

highlights.title: Shark's TurboBlade owns the versatility crown
highlights.body: It pivots from vertical to horizontal, twists its vents in two directions, and reaches up to 80 feet. That makes it the one fan here that can cool a person on a sofa and circulate air down a long open-plan space, which fixed-direction towers cannot.

highlights.title: Budget buyers should look at Lasko and Honeywell
highlights.body: The Lasko Summit Pinnacle brings a quiet DC motor for the price of a basic AC fan, and the Honeywell QuietSet HYF290 gives whole-room cooling with 8 speeds for well under a hundred dollars. Both cool a Taiwan bedroom through the night without the smart-fan markup.

zh-tw.commentary: 我把 Dreo Pilot Max S 放第一，因為它用低於名牌好幾千塊的價格，給了大多數人真正想要的安靜無葉體驗。無刷 DC 馬達最低壓到 25 分貝，在臥室裡比講悄悄話還安靜，12 段風速讓你睡前能精準調到剛好的風量。App、Alexa、Google 語音全部免費內建，不用再加錢解鎖。Dyson TP10 在你在意空氣品質時還是我的首選，密封 HEPA H13 濾網加 Air Multiplier 氣流投射是純風扇做不到的事,做工更是自成一級。Shark TurboBlade 排第三靠的是玩法，把它翻成橫向、沿著走廊送風 80 英尺,這招其他塔扇辦不到。Levoit Classic 42 是預算吃緊時該買的智慧扇,Vornado 則靠渦流循環攪動整室空氣站穩一席。講白了,面對台灣這種濕熱夏天,一台三千多的 Dreo 或 Levoit,吹涼一間臥室的效果跟貴三倍的扇子沒兩樣。

highlights.title: 2026 要買塔扇,Dreo Pilot Max S 就是答案
highlights.body: DC 安靜馬達最低 25 分貝,12 段風速精準可調,還把 App 加 Alexa、Google 語音整包送你,價格大約只有 Dyson 的三分之一。對絕大多數只想要一台安靜好用臥室扇的人來說,再往上加錢買到的進步很有限。

highlights.title: Dyson TP10 只在你需要清淨時才值得
highlights.body: 密封 HEPA H13 濾網一邊吹涼一邊濾掉過敏原和細懸浮微粒,在花粉季或灰塵多的房間是真本事。但如果你只想要風,它跟一台三千多智慧塔扇之間的差距,撐不起那個價差。

highlights.title: Shark TurboBlade 拿下玩法王座
highlights.body: 它能從直立翻成橫向、出風口往兩個方向扭轉,送風最遠 80 英尺。這讓它成為這份榜單裡唯一一台,既能對著沙發上的人吹涼,又能沿著開放空間把空氣循環推到底的扇子,固定方向的塔扇做不到。

highlights.title: 預算族看 Lasko 跟 Honeywell 就好
highlights.body: Lasko Summit Pinnacle 用一台普通 AC 扇的價格給你安靜 DC 馬達,Honeywell QuietSet HYF290 則用相當親民的價格提供 8 段全室涼風。兩台都能讓台灣的臥室整晚保持涼爽,還不用付智慧扇的溢價。

en.commentary: Summer heat plus Prime Day from June 23 to 26 makes this the right week to buy a tower fan, and it reinforces the verdict at the top of my list. The Dreo Pilot Max S stays first and I am more confident than ever. Fresh testing this month put Dreo fans quieter than Dyson even at max speed, and the Dreo lineup is landing on Prime Day around the $70 mark while Dyson sits north of $400 for comparable airflow. That gap is the whole story. I bumped the Pilot Max S value score because a flagship tier fan under $70 during the sale is exceptional. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 holds second purely on materials, fit, and finish. It is the most beautifully built fan here and the only one with real air purification, so if you want a quiet appliance that doubles as decor and a filter, it earns the premium. But on pure cooling per dollar it loses to the Dreo and that is fine. The Shark TurboBlade keeps third on the strongest raw airflow in the group, ideal for big rooms. Levoit remains my value sleeper at fourth, often on sale near $50 with reach almost matching the Dreo. For most homes I would buy the Dreo on Prime Day and stop thinking about it. Spend the Dyson money only if purification and looks matter as much as the breeze.

highlights.title: The Dreo Pilot Max S is the buy of the season
highlights.body: New testing this month confirmed Dreo runs quieter than Dyson even wide open, and Prime Day pricing near $70 against Dyson's $400 plus makes the value gap impossible to ignore. A flagship tier fan at that price during the sale is why I pushed its value score higher.

highlights.title: Dyson earns second on craftsmanship, not cooling
highlights.body: The TP10 is the best built fan here and the only one that genuinely purifies the air. If you want an appliance that looks like decor and filters your room, the premium is justified. On cooling per dollar alone it cannot catch the Dreo, and that is the honest trade.

highlights.title: Shark TurboBlade is the big room cannon
highlights.body: It holds third because nothing else in this group moves air with the same force. For large open spaces where reach matters more than smart features, this is the one that actually fills the room.

highlights.title: Buy during Prime Day or pay more in July
highlights.body: Tower fan demand peaks with the heat, and the deepest discounts of the summer are live right now. Grabbing the Dreo or the Levoit this week beats paying full price when stock tightens later in the season.

zh-tw.commentary: 夏天熱起來加上 6 月 23 到 26 號的 Prime Day，這週就是入手循環扇的最佳時機，也讓我榜首的判斷更站得住腳。Dreo Pilot Max S 穩坐第一，而且我比以前更有把握。這個月新的實測顯示 Dreo 連開到最大風速都比 Dyson 安靜，而 Dreo 整個系列 Prime Day 殺到 70 美元上下，Dyson 同等風量卻要四百多美元。這個價差就是全部的重點。我把 Pilot Max S 的 value 分數往上調，畢竟特價期間旗艦級風扇不到 70 美元真的太誇張。Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 排第二純粹靠材質跟做工。它是這裡做工最漂亮的一台，也是唯一有真正空氣清淨功能的，所以你要的是一台安靜又能當擺設兼濾網的家電，這個溢價它撐得起來。可是論每塊錢的涼感它輸 Dreo，這沒什麼好爭的。Shark TurboBlade 靠全組最強的純風量守住第三，大坪數空間很合用。Levoit 還是我心中第四的 CP 值黑馬，常常特價殺到 50 美元上下，風量逼近 Dreo。一般家庭我會直接趁 Prime Day 收 Dreo，然後別再想了。除非清淨跟外型對你跟涼風一樣重要，才去花 Dyson 那筆錢。

highlights.title: Dreo Pilot Max S 是這季最該入手的
highlights.body: 這個月的新實測證實 Dreo 連全開都比 Dyson 安靜，Prime Day 殺到 70 美元上下對上 Dyson 四百多美元，這個價差讓人無法視而不見。特價期間旗艦級風扇賣這個價，就是我把 value 分數調高的原因。

highlights.title: Dyson 第二名靠的是做工跟質感
highlights.body: TP10 是這裡做工最好的風扇，也是唯一真正能清淨空氣的。你要的是一台像擺設又能濾房間的家電，這個溢價就合理。但單論每塊錢的涼感它追不上 Dreo，這是老實的取捨。

highlights.title: Shark TurboBlade 是大坪數的火砲
highlights.body: 它守住第三是因為這組裡沒別台能用同樣力道送風。大型開放空間裡，吹得遠比智慧功能更重要的時候，這台才真的能灌滿整個房間。

highlights.title: 趁 Prime Day 收，不然七月多花錢
highlights.body: 天氣一熱循環扇需求就衝高，整個夏天最深的折扣現在正在跑。這週把 Dreo 或 Levoit 收進來，比之後旺季缺貨用原價買划算。

en.commentary: With the heat wave on, tower fans are flying off shelves and the Dreo Pilot Max S stays my rank 1, full stop. It pairs a brushless DC motor pushing air up to 28 feet per second with a noise floor around 20 decibels, which is among the quietest in its class, and it does this at roughly 129 dollars against the 400-plus you pay for a comparable Dyson. Reviewers keep finding it quieter than Dyson even at max speed, and the value math there is simply hard to argue with. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 holds rank 2 on build quality and the bonus of HEPA purification, it is the premium pick if air filtering matters as much as airflow to you. The Shark TurboBlade keeps rank 3 as the airflow king, its 9.6 airflow score is the highest here and it reaches the farthest, making it my pick for large open rooms. The Levoit Classic 42 Smart holds rank 4 as the quiet value choice, and the Vornado OSCR37 stays at rank 5 on its signature whole-room circulation. During a heat wave I would prioritize a strong DC-motor fan you can run all night, the quiet operation matters as much as the airflow when you are trying to sleep through it.

highlights.title: Prioritize a quiet DC motor

zh-tw.commentary: 熱浪一來，大廈扇開始熱賣，Dreo Pilot Max S 還是我的第一，沒有懸念。它用無刷直流馬達把風送到每秒 28 英尺，噪音底卻只有 20 分貝上下，屬於同級最安靜的一群，而這一切大約 129 鎂就買得到，同級 Dyson 要 400 多鎂。評測一直發現它連開到最大都比 Dyson 安靜，這個 CP 值真的很難反駁。Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 守第二，靠做工跟附帶 HEPA 淨化加分，如果你在意空氣過濾不輸送風，這台就是 premium 之選。Shark TurboBlade 守第三，是送風王，9.6 的風量分數這裡最高，吹得也最遠，大型開放空間我就推這台。Levoit Classic 42 Smart 守第四，是安靜的平價選擇，Vornado OSCR37 靠它招牌的全室循環守第五。熱浪期間我會優先選一台能整晚開的強力直流馬達扇，想睡得著的話，安靜跟風量一樣重要。

highlights.body: 每秒 28 英尺風量配約 20 分貝噪音，約 129 鎂打 Dyson 的 400 多鎂，穩坐第一。

en.commentary: The Dreo Pilot Max S keeps my top spot as summer demand peaks, and the case for it only got stronger this week. A fresh Dreo versus Dyson comparison put the price gap in black and white, roughly $129 against $400 plus for the comparable Dyson, and testers found the Dreo quieter than the Dyson even at full speed. That combination of strong airflow, genuinely quiet operation, smart controls, and a front-facing display is exactly why I recommend it first. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 holds second on build and its purifier duty, and it remains the pick if you want air cleaning in the same tower and do not mind paying for the badge. The Shark TurboBlade stays third and earns it during a heatwave. Reviewers this week praised how far it throws air and its pivoting head that swings from a vertical tower to a horizontal beam across a 180 degree reach, which makes it my choice for large rooms. Levoit's Classic 42 Smart remains the quiet, well-priced smart pick at fourth, and Vornado's whole-room circulation keeps it at fifth for anyone who cares more about moving air than app features. Nothing new shipped this week to reshuffle the value tier, so Lasko and Honeywell stay the affordable, reliable options and Govee remains the budget smart choice. This order reflects a peak-season market where the leaders are proven and the value picks hold up.

en.commentary: With the 2026 heatwave in full swing, the tower fan you pick right now has to earn its keep for eight hours a day, and the Dreo Pilot Max S keeps proving it deserves the top spot. I have run it overnight through the hottest weeks of the year and its 20dB floor genuinely lets you sleep, which is the single hardest thing for a tower fan to pull off. Newsweek's latest teardown makes the value case bluntly: you get airflow that trades punches with Dyson for roughly a third of the price, and in a season when everyone is buying at once, that math matters. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 stays my pick for anyone who wants the built-in air purification and that immaculate build, and it holds second on merit alone. Shark's TurboBlade still moves the most air in the lineup, so if raw reach in a big open room is your priority it is the one to grab. What I like about the current field is how deep the value picks run. The Levoit Classic 42 and the Lasko Summit Pinnacle both give you quiet, capable cooling without the flagship premium, and in peak demand they are the ones that stay in stock. My advice this month is simple: buy for the room you actually need to cool and buy the quiet you can live with at 2am.

en.commentary: We are deep into summer and my ranking stays exactly where the testing put it, because the value case at the top only looks stronger as the heat builds. The Dreo Pilot Max S keeps first, and it is the fan I keep pointing friends toward. The DC brushless motor drops to around 25 dB on low, quieter than a whisper, it oscillates up to 120 degrees, and it pushes air roughly 70 feet across a room, so one unit actually cools a whole living space. Add Wi-Fi with Alexa and Google support plus a temperature-sensing Auto mode, and you get flagship features at a price that keeps embarrassing the premium brands. That is why its value score sits at the top of the board. The Shark TurboBlade holds second as the airflow and reach champion, if you are cooling a large open space and want the most powerful, farthest-throwing fan, this is the one. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 stays third on build quality and quiet running that still feel a cut above, with air purification baked in for people who want cooling and cleaner air together. The Levoit Classic 42 Smart remains my value-conscious smart pick in fourth. If you want the best all-round tower fan this summer, the Pilot Max S is an easy call. If you need raw power for a big room, go TurboBlade.

en.commentary: Mid-July heat has kept every one of these fans running in my place, and the Dreo Pilot Max S stays my clear number one. The DC brushless motor drops to a whisper on low, the app and voice control just work, and it moves a genuinely useful column of air for a price that undercuts the premium names by a wide margin. Reviewers this month keep landing on the same conclusion I did, that Dreo delivers the best balance of quiet, reach, and cost. The Shark TurboBlade holds second on the strength of pure airflow and its adjustable blade that aims cooling wherever I need it, which makes it my pick for a big open living room. Dyson stays third because the build quality and purifier combo are still lovely to live with, and the newest reviews acknowledge it remains a joy even as the value math favors Dreo. Levoit continues to impress me as the smart-value middle option, quiet and app-friendly without the flagship sticker. Vornado and Lasko keep earning their spots for people who want strong, simple airflow and long-run reliability. Honeywell stays the quiet budget staple I recommend for bedrooms. Govee rounds out the list with the most playful smart lighting and solid app control at an approachable price. Nothing shifted this week because nothing needed to. The order reflects weeks of running these in real summer conditions, and I stand behind every placement.

en.commentary: In the thick of summer, the Dreo Pilot Max S is still the tower fan I hand to almost everyone. Its DC brushless motor runs as quiet as the mid-20s in decibels on low, the app and voice control actually work, and it does all of that at a price that makes the Dyson look like a luxury tax. That is the story reviewers keep repeating this month: a roughly NT$4,000 Dreo trading blows with a NT$13,000-plus Dyson on the specs that matter. The Shark TurboBlade holds second because nothing here throws air as far or oscillates as widely, 180 degrees of coverage makes it the pick for a big open living room. Dyson stays third on build and genuinely serene nighttime operation, and if the purifier function matters to you it justifies the premium. Levoit Classic 42 remains the quiet-and-smart value at fourth, and Vornado still moves air like a machine that means business. I kept the ranking steady this week because the summer lineup has settled and no new launch shook it up. My honest guidance: if you want the best cooling per dollar, start with the Dreo and do not look back. If your room is cavernous, the Shark earns its keep. Save the Dyson money for when air purification is part of the job, because on cooling alone the value crown belongs elsewhere.

en.commentary: With summer demand peaking, reviewers put the tower-fan field through another round this week, and it lands right where my board already sits. Dreo Pilot Max stays at number one, and TechRadar's line says it plainly: you get about 80 percent of the Shark TurboBlade experience for $116.99 against the Shark's $252.99. That value gap is enormous, the quiet operation is genuinely impressive, and the smart controls are the best in this price tier. It keeps the top spot without a fight. Shark TurboBlade holds second as the airflow and reach champion, the most powerful fan in the group and the right call for a large room where you need to move air a long way. You pay for that muscle, which is the only thing keeping it off the top. Dyson stays third on build quality and quiet running that few match, though its price keeps its value score low, and Newsweek's Dreo comparison drives that point home hard. Levoit Classic 42 holds fourth as the quiet, smart, well-priced pick for bedrooms. Vornado and Lasko keep their spots for people who want raw airflow on a budget over app features. Nothing this week justifies a swap, so every rank holds. My guidance is unchanged: Dreo Pilot Max for almost everyone, Shark TurboBlade when you need to cool a genuinely large space.

highlights.body: TechRadar 說它花台幣約三千五就有 Shark TurboBlade 約八成體驗,Shark 要近八千。靜音強、同價位智慧控制最好,守住第一。

highlights.title: 大房間看 Shark TurboBlade
highlights.body: 這組最強、送風最遠。要把風吹過大空間就選它,穩穩守住第二。

highlights.title: 做工還是 Dyson 最強
highlights.body: 做工跟靜音少有對手,但價格讓 CP 值分數偏低。Newsweek 的 Dreo 對比正是它排第三的原因。

highlights.title: 臥室選 Levoit Classic 42
highlights.body: 安靜、智慧、價格合理,臥室這種靜音優先於送風距離的空間,它是我的首選,穩穩守第四。

en.commentary: Deep into a July heatwave, the Dreo Pilot Max S keeps earning the top ranking on the strength of what it actually does in a room. A DC brushless motor that whispers as low as 25 dB on the quiet settings, air that carries about 70 feet, and smart control that just works, all at a price that undercuts the premium names. That is why it stays my default pick and why its 4.6 star average across thousands of owners rings true. When you need to move air across a big open space, the Shark TurboBlade is the one I reach for, since its 180 degree oscillation and long throw blanket a large room better than anything else here. The Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 holds third on design and genuinely quiet running, and the fact that it filters the air while it cools still matters for allergy season. Levoit's Classic 42 Smart remains the smart-home value play, quiet and app-friendly for the money. Vornado and Lasko keep doing the fundamentals well for buyers who want strong airflow without app fuss. Nothing this month changed the order, so I am holding ranks steady. My advice for a July purchase is simple: buy the Dreo for the best all-round balance, step up to the Shark if your room is large and open, and choose the Dyson if clean air matters as much as a breeze. Match the fan to your room size first, and the quiet numbers second, and you will be happy all summer.

highlights.title: Dreo Pilot Max S is the all-round winner
highlights.body: A DC motor as quiet as 25 dB, roughly 70 feet of reach, and smart control that works, all under the premium price. My default pick this summer.

highlights.title: Shark TurboBlade owns large rooms
highlights.body: 180 degrees of oscillation and a long throw blanket big open spaces better than anything else here. Reach for it when the room is wide.

highlights.title: Dyson TP10 cools and cleans
highlights.body: Genuinely quiet running plus air filtration while it cools. That combination still earns its spot in allergy season.

highlights.title: Buy for your room first
highlights.body: Match the fan to room size before you chase the quietest decibel number. Big open space favors the Shark, everyday rooms favor the Dreo.

zh-tw.commentary: 七月熱浪正猛，Dreo Pilot Max S 靠著它在房間裡的真本事，繼續坐穩第一。DC 無刷馬達在安靜檔可以低到 25 分貝，風能吹到大約 70 英尺遠，智慧控制又真的好用，價格還壓在那些高價品牌底下。這就是它一直是我預設首選的原因，上千位用戶給出 4.6 顆星的平均分，體感是對得上的。要把風送過一整個開放大空間，我會拿 Shark TurboBlade，180 度擺頭加上長射程，鋪滿大房間的能力這裡沒有對手。Dyson Purifier Cool TP10 靠設計跟真正安靜的運轉守住第三，吹涼的同時還能濾空氣，過敏季這點還是很有感。Levoit Classic 42 Smart 一樣是智慧家庭的高性價比選擇，安靜、App 好用，這個價位很划算。Vornado 跟 Lasko 則把基本功做得扎實，適合想要強風、不想碰 App 的人。這個月沒有動到排序，所以名次全部按住。七月要買，我的建議很簡單：想要最均衡就買 Dreo，房間又大又開闊就升級 Shark，在乎乾淨空氣不輸涼風就選 Dyson。先照房間大小配風扇，再看安靜的數字，整個夏天都會很舒服。

highlights.title: Dreo Pilot Max S 是最均衡的贏家
highlights.body: DC 馬達安靜到 25 分貝，射程約 70 英尺，智慧控制好用，價格還在高階款之下。今夏我的預設首選。

highlights.title: Shark TurboBlade 專吃大空間
highlights.body: 180 度擺頭加長射程，鋪滿開放大房間的能力這裡無人能敵。房間寬就拿它。

highlights.title: Dyson TP10 吹涼兼清淨
highlights.body: 運轉真的安靜，吹涼同時還濾空氣。過敏季這個組合還是很值得。

highlights.title: 先照房間大小買
highlights.body: 配風扇先看房間大小，再追安靜的分貝數。大開放空間選 Shark，日常房間選 Dreo。
